query: Berkshire Hathaway PacifiCorp Oregon wildfires 2020 appeals court ruling outcome 2026
a_issues: Content A does contain the key outcome via reliable sources (Statesman Journal and a Reuters-derived Claims Journal repost): the Oregon appeals court ruled for PacifiCorp, found the class-action handling/jury instruction improper and prejudicial, and reversed/remanded the case, which could reduce damages. However, the top result is PacifiCorp's own litigation page, which is self-interested and mixes broader background rather than clearly stating the 2026 ruling. One Reuters item is only a LinkedIn post rather than the original article.
b_issues: Content B also contains the key outcome, mainly through Reuters syndications (Sahm Capital and MarketScreener): PacifiCorp won the appeal; the court said the trial judge erred in letting the case proceed as a class action / in instructing jurors they could assume trial evidence applied to all class members; the ruling could reduce liability. However, the top result is an unreliable-looking/possibly fabricated local-news-style page (nationaltoday.com/Portland Today) with noisy irrelevant text. B is also more dependent on reposts/syndication than primary reporting.
reasoning: Both A and B provide enough accurate facts to answer the question correctly: in 2026 the Oregon Court of Appeals ruled in favor of Berkshire Hathaway's PacifiCorp in the 2020 Oregon wildfire litigation, holding that the trial court improperly handled the case as a class action / gave erroneous jury instructions, and reversed/remanded the verdict, potentially reducing or wiping out substantial damages. This matches the anchor Reuters report. A has one solid direct local source plus a Reuters-derived repost; B has multiple Reuters-derived reposts saying the same thing. Neither set has a decisive factual advantage, though each has some source-quality noise, so they enable the answer about equally well.
